mutations.change: Consume a redundant pickup even though every granted resource is already at its cap.
mutations.reason: Classic pickup consumption is conditional on a useful state change; deletion at cap loses map state and item accounting.

mutations.change: Add pickup ammo without applying the authoritative normal or backpack-derived cap.
mutations.reason: Unbounded inventory violates reviewed gameplay limits and makes otherwise inert pickups alter authoritative state.

mutations.change: Grant the backpack pickup event without doubling each relational ammunition capacity.
mutations.reason: Backpack capacity is durable inventory state needed by subsequent pickup and replay outcomes.

mutations.change: Allow selecting an owned ammo weapon while its required ammunition count is zero.
mutations.reason: The reviewed selection rule must keep the usable current weapon and cannot defer an impossible attack state.

mutations.change: Replace the selected weapon immediately and skip lowering and raising weapon states.
mutations.reason: Weapon and muzzle state timing is authoritative gameplay visible to replay and frame hashing.

mutations.change: Use no independent horizontal spread pair for each hitscan pellet and consume fewer random values.
mutations.reason: Random cursor order is observable by every later attack and must match the independently reviewed draw schedule.

mutations.change: Replace table-driven damage randomization with a fixed five-point hitscan result.
mutations.reason: Damage is derived from the ordered Doom random table and fixed damage produces a divergent deterministic replay.

mutations.change: Choose a farther shootable target instead of the stable nearest valid intercept.
mutations.reason: Hitscan must terminate at the first exact intercept and cannot penetrate a closer solid actor.

mutations.change: Spawn a projectile with zero forward momentum despite an otherwise valid firing transition.
mutations.reason: Database-owned fixed momentum is required for deterministic motion and eventual collision.

mutations.change: Retain an impacted projectile after emitting its collision event and applying damage.
mutations.reason: An impact consumes a projectile once; retention permits repeated damage and duplicate events on later tics.

mutations.change: Apply radius damage through blocking geometry without an exact line-of-effect test.
mutations.reason: Occluded actors must not receive rocket or barrel splash even when Euclidean distance lies inside the radius.

mutations.change: Mark a killed barrel exploded but suppress its own deterministic radius damage expansion.
mutations.reason: E1M1 barrel groups rely on ordered chain damage and each newly destroyed barrel must explode exactly once.

mutations.change: Evaluate only one pellet for a shotgun shot while still consuming one shell.
mutations.reason: Seven independently spread and damaged pellets are part of the fixed weapon definition and RNG schedule.

mutations.change: Fire an ammunition weapon without subtracting the definition's exact per-shot cost.
mutations.reason: Ammo ownership and later no-ammo behavior depend on atomic consumption at the firing transition.

mutations.change: Advance the deterministic random cursor when an attack is rejected for insufficient ammunition.
mutations.reason: Rejected actions perform no attack draws; cursor drift changes unrelated later spread and damage.

mutations.change: Apply the same consumed map pickup a second time to one player session.
mutations.reason: Pickup consumption is authoritative mobj state and must prevent duplicate health, ammo, and item events.

mutations.change: Replace stronger blue armor with weaker green armor solely because the pickup was touched.
mutations.reason: Armor set pickups only improve protection; downgrading changes both points and damage absorption class.

mutations.change: Add healing without the item's reviewed one-hundred or two-hundred health ceiling.
mutations.reason: Distinct medical and bonus caps determine both resulting health and whether the pickup is consumed.

mutations.change: Permit a newly launched projectile to collide with its owner before leaving the muzzle.
mutations.reason: Launch ownership exclusion prevents immediate impact while later splash may still damage the owner.

mutations.change: Advance weapon refire from elapsed wall time rather than fixed logical tic state durations.
mutations.reason: Save, rewind, retry, and replay require weapon state advancement to depend only on ordered logical tics.

mutations.change: Apply simultaneous splash victims in insertion order instead of stable authoritative identifier order.
mutations.reason: Barrel chains and event ordinals must remain identical under physically irrelevant row insertion order.

mutations.change: Dispatch pickups and barrels from literal E1M1 thing identifiers in procedural branches.
mutations.reason: The implementation contract is relational and map-independent; embedding fixture identifiers is evaluator coupling.
